title: 第 93 節
en: 2 Ait. Ar. Ill, 1, 4.
3 The Kashmir MS. reads etasmin pra;ze. The self here is meant for the body, and yet it seems to be different from ^arira.
4 The Kashmir MS. writes antastha without visarga, while it is otherwise most careful in writing all sibilants.
5 -Sakalya, as we saw, told his disciples that there were three classes only, not four. Comm. The Kashmir MS. reads traya;« tv eva na ityetat proktam.
6 The Kashmir MS. reads sapta vimsaAis ka. jatani.
[3] S 258 aitareya-ar a.nyak a.
Second Khanda.
1. Next comes Kau^^aravya :
2. There are 360 syllables (vowels), 360 sibilants (consonants), 360 groups.
3. What we called syllables are the days, what we called sibilants are the nights, what we called groups are the junctions of days and nights. So far with regard to the gods (the days).
4. Now with regard to the body. The syllables which we explained mythologically, are physiologically the bones ; the sibilants which we explained mythologically, are physiologically the marrow.
5. Marrow is the real breath (life), for marrow is seed, and without breath (life) seed is not sown. Or when it is sown without breath (life), it will decay, it will not grow.
6. The groups which we explained mythologically, are physiologically the joints.

7. Of that triad, viz. bones, marrow, and joints, there are 540 (parts) on this side (the right), and 540 on that side (the left). They make 1080 together, and 1080 are the rays of the sun. They make the EWhati verses and the day (of the Manavrata) x.
8. Thus that self which consists of sight, hearing, metre, mind, and speech is like unto the syllables.

9. He who knows this self which consists of sight, hearing, metre, mind, and speech, as like unto syllables, obtains union, likeness, or nearness with the syllables, has sons and cattle, and lives his full age.
1 There are in the Mahavrata eighty tristichs of Br/hatis, and as each Brzhati is decreed to consist of thirty-six syllables, ten would give 360 syllables, and three times ten, 1080. Comm.
Ill ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 3 KHANDA, IO. 259 Third Khaa^da.
1. Badhva1 says, there are four persons (to be meditated on and worshipped).
2. The person of the body, the person of the metres, the person of the Veda, and the Great person.
3. What we call the person of the body is this corporeal self. Its essence is the incorporeal conscious self.
4. What we call the person of the metres is this collection of letters (the Veda). Its essence is the vowel a.
5. What we call the person of the Veda is (the mind) by which we know the Vedas, the i?zg-veda, Ya^ur-veda, and Sama-veda. Its essence is Brahman 2 (m.)
6. Therefore let one chose a Brahman-priest who is full of Brahman (the Veda), and is able to see any flaw in the sacrifice.
7. What we call the Great person is the year, which causes some beings to fall together, and causes others to grow up. Its essence is yonder sun.
8. One should know that the incorporeal conscious self and yonder sun are both one and the same. Therefore the sun appears to every man singly (and differently).

9. This has also been declared by a Rzshi (Rv. I, 115, Oiio. ' The bright face of the gods arose, the eye of Mitra, Varmza, and Agni ; it filled heaven and earth 1 Instead of Badhya, the commentary and the Kashmir MS. read Badhva.
2 Hira7/yagarbha, with whom he who knows the Veda becomes identified. Comm.
S 2 26O AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
and the sky, — the sun is the self of all that rests and moves.'
zh: 2 艾特里亞歐義書 III, 1, 4。
3 喀什米爾手稿本讀作 etasmin pra;ze。此處的「自我」是指身體，然而它似乎有別於 śarīra。
喀什米爾寫本將 antastha 寫作不含 visarga，雖然在書寫所有的喉音方面，該寫本在其他方面最為謹慎。
5 -如我們所見，沙卡利亞告訴他的弟子們只有三個種姓，而非四個。注疏。喀什米爾手稿本讀作 traya；「tv eva na ityetat proktam」。
6 喀什米爾抄本讀作 sapta vimsaAis ka. jatani。
[3] S 258 愛達羅耶奧義書（Aitareya-Aranyaka）。
第二篇章
其次是考沙羅婆耶（Kau^^aravya）：
有三百六十個音節（元音）、三百六十個咝音（輔音）、三百六十個音組。
3. 我們所謂的音節是諸日，我們所謂的音擦音是諸夜，我們所謂的音群是日夜之交接。至此關於諸提婆（諸日）之事。
4. 至於身體方面。我們神話學上所解釋的音節，在生理學上乃是骨骼；我們神話學上所解釋的齒音，在生理學上乃是骨髓。
5. 骨髓乃真實的呼吸（生命），蓋骨髓即種子，無呼吸（生命）種子不得播撒。或當未有呼吸（生命）而播撒時，種子將腐敗，將不得生長。
6. 我們從神話學角度解釋的那些群組，在生理學上就是各個關節。
7. 在那個三組物質——即骨、骨髓與關節——之中，此側（右側）有 540 個（部分），彼側（左側）亦有 540 個。合計為 1080，而太陽之光線亦為 1080。它們構成了《EWhati》詩篇以及（Manavrata 的）白日 x。
8. 因此，那由視覺、聽覺、韻律、心念與言語所組成的自我，就如同音節一般。
凡知此自我者，知其由視覺、聽覺、韻律、心意與言語所組成，如同音節（syllables）之相似者，則得與音節相合、相似或相近，具有子嗣與牲畜，並享盡其天年。
在《摩訶毗陀》（Mahavrata）中有八十首婆利訶體（Brzhatis）的三句詩節，而每一婆利訶體按規定應包含三十六個音節，十個將得到三百六十個音節，三倍的十即為一千零八十。註疏。
III 阿蘭亞卡 2 阿迪雅亞 3 坎達 10 第259頁 第三坎達
巴德瓦（Badhva）說，有四位人物（應當被冥想與崇拜）。
2. 身體之人、韻律之人、吠陀之人，及偉大之人。
3. 我們所謂的身體之人（the person of the body）就是這個物質自我。其本質乃是非物質的有意識自我。
4. 我們所謂韻律之人格，乃是這些字母（吠陀）的集合。其本質乃是元音 a。
5. 我們所謂的吠陀人格乃是（心智），藉此我們認識吠陀——梨俱吠陀、夜柔吠陀和娑摩吠陀。其本質是梵（Brahman）。
因此，應當選擇一位充滿梵（吠陀）之知的婆羅門祭司，他能夠洞察祭祀中的任何瑕疵。
我們所謂的偉大人物就是歲月，它使某些生命衰落在一起，也使他者成長起來。其本質就是那邊的太陽。
8. 人應當知曉，無形的有意識的自我與彼處的太陽乃是一體無二。是故太陽對於每個人都各自顯現（並有所不同）。
9. 一位婆羅門聖者（Rshi）也曾如此宣言（《梨俱吠陀》I, 115, 奧義書註），「提婆諸神的光輝容顏升起，那是密多羅（Mitra）、伐樓那（Varuna）及阿耆尼（Agni）的眼睛；它充滿了天地」。註：註疏及克什米爾手稿作「伐樓那」（Badhva），而非「跋提亞」（Badhya）。
2 希拉尼亞伽爾巴（Hiranyagarbha），認識吠陀者與之合一。注疏。
第2卷260 《愛達羅耶奧義書》（Aitareya-Aranyaka）
而天空——太陽是一切靜止者與運動者的自我。'
